records.content: This section will collect key analytical questions relevant to the 2026 DRC Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak response.

This resource list is maintained by the Ebola Outbreak Analytics CoP. Contributions are welcome from anyone working on the 2026 DRC Ebola Bundibugyo response.

Formatting guide

Maintainers Guide

Open a GitHub Issue using our structured form:

Suggest a Resource

Fill in the fields — title, URL, type, section, authors, organisation, date, and a short description. A GitHub Action will create a draft page and open a Pull Request. A maintainer will review, adjust placement if needed, and merge.

Create a .md file in the appropriate section directory (e.g. docs/resources/dashboards/my-resource.md) and add an {include} directive to the section’s index file (e.g. docs/resources/tools-index.md). Use the format below.

Every resource page should include these mandatory metadata fields in a standardized header:# Resource Title

Type · **Organisation** · Authors · Date

Description of the resource — as much or as little as needed.

[Visit resource](https://...)

Field

Required

Example

Title

Yes

# INRB UMIE Dashboard

Type

Yes

Dashboard, Report, Tool, Dataset, Package

Organisation

Yes

Imperial College London

Authors

Yes

Ruth McCabe et al.

Date

Yes

2026-05-20

Link

Yes

[Visit resource](https://...)

Description

Yes

Free-text Markdown

Beyond the header, the page body is free-text Markdown. See the 

Formatting guide for examples of tables, code blocks, admonitions, math, and more.

We curate resources that are:

Relevant to the 2026 DRC Ebola Bundibugyo outbreak response

Publicly accessible (or with a clear access path)

Actionable — dashboards, datasets, tools, reports, and packages that responders and researchers can use

If a resource doesn’t fit an existing section, mention that in your issue or PR — we can create new sections as the response evolves.
This page shows what’s available when writing resource pages. Everything below is valid MyST Markdown and renders on the site.

Every resource page starts with this:# Resource Title

Type · **Organisation** · Authors · Date

Description text.

[Visit resource](https://...)
Bold text, italic text, and inline code.

External: 

WHO Collaboratory

Internal cross-reference: 

Contributing

Bullet list:

First item

Second item

Nested item

Numbered list:

First step

Second step

Third step

Parameter

Estimate

Source

Incubation period

2–21 days

WHO

Case fatality rate

25–34%

McCabe et al.

Serial interval

15.3 days

Epiparameter

R example with syntax highlighting:library(epiparameter)
ebola_params <- epiparameter_db(disease = "ebola")
plot(ebola_params)

Python example:import pandas as pd
df = pd.read_csv("outbreak_data.csv")
df.groupby("health_zone")["cases"].sum()

Note

Use notes for general information or context.

Tip

Use tips for practical advice — e.g. how to access a dataset or interpret a dashboard.

Warning

Use warnings for caveats — e.g. preliminary data, known limitations, or access restrictions.

Important

Use important for critical context that readers should not miss.

![Alt text](path/to/image.png)

Images can be placed in an assets/ directory alongside the resource file or in the top-level assets/ folder.

This outbreak represents a significant public health emergency requiring coordinated international response.

WHO Situation Report, June 2026

Inline math: R_0 = 1.8

Display math:R_t = R_0 \cdot S(t) / N
The case fatality rate varies by setting.

Use --- to separate sections visually, as shown throughout this page.

See McCabe et al. (2026) for a detailed breakdown by health zone.

A curated, community-maintained collection of resources supporting the response to the 2026 DRC Ebola Bundibugyo virus disease outbreak. This site is maintained by the Ebola Outbreak Analytics CoP within the 

WHO Collaboratory.

The easiest way to contribute is to 

open a GitHub Issue. Just fill in the form — no coding needed. A maintainer will review and add it to the site.

See 

Contributing for more details.

Ebola Outbreak Analytics - CoP Resources

Situation Updates

Resources and tools

Dashboards

Epidemiological Parameter Estimates

Literature

Therapeutics & Vaccines

Data

Linelist

Epidemiological Parameter Estimates

Mobility

Humanitarian

Emerging evidence

Outbreak Size

Risk of Spread

Therapeutics & Vaccines

Analytical questions

Community meeting materials

Contributing

Formatting guide

Maintainers Guide

A community member opens a GitHub Issue using the “Suggest a Resource” form

A GitHub Action creates a .md file in the appropriate section directory, appends an {include} directive to the section’s index file, and opens a Pull Request

The maintainer reviews the PR — checks that the file is in the right section and the {include} is placed correctly — and merges

A deploy workflow builds and publishes the updated site

For resources submitted as “Other / New section”, the file goes to docs/_incoming/ and no index file is modified — the maintainer places it manually.
Technical contributors may open PRs with new .md files or edits to existing ones. The maintainer adds an {include} directive to the appropriate section index file during review.

myst.yml                    ← site config + TOC (references index files only)
assets/
  logo.png                  ← site logo and static assets
docs/
  intro.md                  ← landing page
  contributing.md
  maintainers.md
  analytical-questions.md
  _incoming/                ← staging area for automation-created files
  resources/                ← all contributed content
    tools-index.md          ← section index: Resources and tools
    data-index.md           ← section index: Data
    emerging-evidence-index.md  ← section index: Emerging evidence
    community-meetings-index.md ← section index: Community meeting materials
    dashboards/             ← one .md file per resource
    epi-parameters/
    outbreak-size-estimates/
    risk-of-spread/
    mobility-data/
    humanitarian-data/
    therapeutics-vaccines/

Each section has an index file (e.g. tools-index.md) that uses {include} directives to pull in individual resource files. This means:

All resources in a section render as one continuous page in the sidebar

Individual resource files stay separate for easy editing and version control

The sidebar shows one entry per section, not one per resource

hierarchy.lvl3: Adding a resource to the site
Adding a resource to the site

records.content:
Create the .md file in the appropriate subdirectory (e.g. docs/resources/dashboards/my-resource.md)

Append an include block to the section’s index file:---

```{include} dashboards/my-resource.md
Include paths are relative to the `docs/resources/` directory where the index files live.

### Creating a new section

1. Create a new index file (e.g. `docs/resources/new-section-index.md`):

```markdown
# New Section Name

Brief description of the section.

---

```{include} new-section/first-resource.md
2. Add it to `myst.yml`:

```yaml
- file: docs/resources/new-section-index.md

Important: When creating a new section, also:

Update the section dropdown in .github/ISSUE_TEMPLATE/new-resource.yml

Add the section mapping in .github/scripts/parse-issue.py (SECTION_MAP)
Rearrange the {include} directives in the section’s index file. Move .md files between directories if you want the file paths to match.

pip install jupyter-book
jupyter-book start

The site builds at http://localhost:3000/.

The Collaboratory is collating literature relevant to the Bundibugyo virus disease outbreak. The list is an open accessible sheet assembled to support modelers, epidemiologists, researchers, and public health analysts working on this pathogen and is capturing publications, pre-prints, github repositories, data sets, tools and other outbreak related materials. The list is updated in near real-time as new publications appear. We recognize that some relevant work may still be missed, and we welcome contributions from the community. You can add to the list in the BDBV_Literature sheet in the Community_Submissions section or let us know at 

collaboratory@who​.int.

Access real-time literature list: 

Bundibugyo Virus Disease Literature

For updates on the evolving Bundibugyo virus disease outbreak access the latest situation reports at the following links:

DRC situation reports

Uganda Ebola Updates

WHO Africa Region outbreak situation reports

WHO Disease Outbreak News
Seminars, presentations, and recordings from community meetings.

Dataset · Flowminder · Linus Bengtsson · 2026

Monthly population-scaled inter-health-zone mobility data relevant to outbreak spread. Shorter-term mobility dynamics are in progress.

Contact 

linus​.bengtsson@flowminder​.org for access. Work is underway to release shorter-term mobility dynamics.
